data.title: Implement setup-fixtures.sh: download/cache open-source fixture repos for integration tests
data.description:
## Testing Mode
GREEN

## Description
Implement tests/integration/setup-fixtures.sh — downloads and caches open-source Python and TypeScript projects used as integration test fixtures.

### Fixture targets (chosen for: small size, stable tests, multiple files, permissive license):
1. **python-add-parameter** (Python): Clone https://github.com/pallets/click at a pinned commit (a known small, well-tested Python library with many function definitions and callers). Or use a minimal synthetic fixture that mimics real structure. The fixture must have: 15+ callers across 8+ files, a working test suite (pytest), MIT/BSD license.
   ALTERNATIVE (preferred for stability): Create a synthetic fixture at tests/integration/fixtures/python-add-parameter/ checked into the repo as a minimal Python project with pyproject.toml, 10+ files, multiple callers. This avoids network dependency.

2. **typescript-add-parameter** (TypeScript): Minimal synthetic TypeScript project with tsconfig.json, 8+ files, 15+ callers, package.json with test script.

3. **python-normalize-imports** (Python): Reuse python-add-parameter fixture (import normalization applies to the same codebase).

### Approach:
Given CI reliability concerns, use SYNTHETIC fixtures committed to the repo rather than network clones. Create minimal Python/TypeScript projects that satisfy the integration test requirements without requiring external downloads.

Files to create:
- tests/integration/setup-fixtures.sh (checks if synthetic fixtures exist; for network fixtures, clones them)
- tests/integration/fixtures/python-project/ (synthetic Python project: pyproject.toml, src/, tests/)
- tests/integration/fixtures/typescript-project/ (synthetic TS project: tsconfig.json, src/, package.json)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/integration/setup-fixtures.sh exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x tests/integration/setup-fixtures.sh
- [ ] Synthetic Python fixture exists with Python source files and pyproject.toml
  Verify: test -f tests/integration/fixtures/python-project/pyproject.toml
- [ ] Python fixture has 8+ source files with function definitions and callers
  Verify: find tests/integration/fixtures/python-project -name "*.py" | wc -l | awk '{exit ($1 < 8)}'
- [ ] Synthetic TypeScript fixture exists with tsconfig.json
  Verify: test -f tests/integration/fixtures/typescript-project/tsconfig.json
- [ ] All tests in test-fixture-setup.sh pass
  Verify: bash tests/integration/test-fixture-setup.sh
